tasks.title: Analyze ClaudeCode competitor
tasks.description: Examine the source code located in ../claude-code-sourcemap. Identify its key features, user workflows, and any unique capabilities. Summarize strengths, weaknesses, and notable implementation details that are relevant for our product. Output a concise markdown file named claudecode_analysis.md containing the findings.

tasks.title: Analyze Copilot-CLI competitor
tasks.description: Review the public repository or documentation for copilot-cli. Determine its major features, command-line interface design, integration points, and any performance or usability aspects that differentiate it. Produce a markdown file named copilotcli_analysis.md summarizing the analysis.

tasks.title: Analyze OpenCode competitor
tasks.description: Explore the source code located in ../opencode. Catalog its core functionalities, architecture, and any innovative features. Note gaps or areas where it falls short compared to our product. Write the results to a markdown file called opencode_analysis.md.

tasks.title: Analyze Roocode competitor
tasks.description: Investigate the Roocode project (access method can be a provided repo URL or local copy). Identify its feature set, user experience, and technical approach. Summarize findings in a markdown file named roocode_analysis.md.

tasks.title: Synthesize analyses and create SWARMOUT2.md
tasks.description:
Collect the four analysis markdown files from tasks s1‑s4. Compare their feature sets with our current product, and generate a comprehensive document called SWARMOUT2.md. The document must:
1. List suggested feature additions for our product, citing which competitor provides each feature.
2. Provide a prioritized plan with clear Milestones and detailed tasks required to implement the missing features.
3. Include references to the source competitor analyses.
4. Save SWARMOUT2.md (and optionally the four analysis files) into the directory target/temp/swarmout.
Ensure the output directory exists or is created before writing files.
